raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this statement regarding improper services provided by a moving company and the subsequent mishandling of my dispute by Barclays. 
I entered into a written contract with the moving company for services that included a specific, agreed-upon receiving/delivery date. I possess both the written contract and a receipt confirming these terms. Without my consent, and without providing any notice to me, the moving company unilaterally changed the contracted receiving date. 
When I contacted the company to question this change, I was informed that the altered date was what it was, and I was accused of lying despite my possession of written documentation showing the originally agreed-upon date. At no point did the company acknowledge responsibility for the change, nor did they provide any advance notice or reasonable explanation for altering the contract terms. 
As a result of this issue, I submitted a formal dispute to Barclays. Barclays failed to follow up with me during the dispute process and ultimately closed the case without providing any notification. According to Barclays own stated policy, customers are to receive a letter explaining the reason a dispute is closed. I did not receive any such letter, explanation, or notice. 
In summary, I received no proper notification, no follow-up communication, and no written explanation regarding the closure of my dispute. Both the moving company and Barclays failed to adhere to basic contractual and procedural standards, causing me financial harm and significant inconvenience.
